TimeSnap evidence verification

Verify the moment.
Trust the evidence.

Upload a TimeSnap photo and we will read the Data Matrix on the left strip first. If that cannot be decoded, the scanner falls back to OCR on the vertical TS code, then checks your Firebase registry and produces a clear evidence report.

01 — image scan

Drop a TimeSnap image here

The scanner now checks the left-strip Data Matrix first, then falls back to the vertical TS-XXXXXXX OCR code if needed.

For best results, keep the full left edge visible, including the Data Matrix square and the vertical code strip.

02 — direct lookup

Already able to read the code?

Manual lookup is the quickest option and avoids OCR mistakes caused by blur, crop or glare.

vertical overlay
TS-

Enter the 7 characters after TS-.

Registry status confirms that a code exists. A code match alone cannot prove every pixel in a shared or edited image is unchanged.

No readable TimeSnap code found

The Data Matrix and the left-edge OCR code could not be read reliably. This does not prove the picture is fake or altered — the barcode/code may be blurred, covered, re-compressed or too low contrast. Enter the visible code manually if you can read it.

What visual screening means

This score is deliberately not called an “AI detector”.

From a browser-only page, it is not honest or reliable to claim that a photo is definitely AI-generated, edited, or unedited. The score combines readable-code confidence, registry match status, and an optional exact file-fingerprint comparison when your Firebase record stores a SHA-256 hash.

For forensic image analysis or a real AI-manipulation model, use a protected server-side endpoint or Firebase Cloud Function. Do not expose private AI API keys inside this HTML file.
